5_schedule_change.change: removed the `schedule: cron: "*/30 * * * *"` trigger entirely; workflow_dispatch retained

6_internal_time_budget_architecture.model: createTimeBudget(routeMaxDurationMs, safetyMarginMs, now) -> absolute deadlineMs; remainingBudgetMs/isTimeBudgetExhausted read against it via an injectable clock
6_internal_time_budget_architecture.checked: once per batch-loop iteration (before starting a new query) using a single unified threshold shared with the per-call floor -- an inconsistency between two separate checks was found and fixed by this mission's own tests before being merged

9_serverless_batch_cap.applies_only_when: no batchSizeOverride supplied (the real cron/dispatch path; CLI bootstrap waves are unaffected)

10_checkpoint_strategy.mechanism: persistRotationUpdates([updatedQuery], ...) called immediately after each query finishes, not batched at the end of the loop
10_checkpoint_strategy.budget_state_persisted: once per invocation, unconditionally including on early/partial exit

11_lease_and_atomic_recovery.acquire_function: acquire_openserp_ingestion_lock -- single INSERT...ON CONFLICT...DO UPDATE...WHERE expires_at < now(), row-count-based success detection (no re-read race)
11_lease_and_atomic_recovery.release_function: release_openserp_ingestion_lock -- DELETE...WHERE lock_name=... AND owner_id=..., owner-checked

12_files_modified.change: lease-based lock calls (ownerId + leaseSeconds), passes routeMaxDurationMs through
configurable per-call timeout, EngineCallError classification (HTTP + CLI paths)
rewritten as a thin wrapper over the new lease-based repository
time budget integration, per-unit checkpointing, batch cap, structured outcome_status
new -- RPC wrapper for the 2 atomic lock functions
new -- pure, injectable-clock time budget primitive

new -- HTTP + CLI path outcome classification
new -- PGlite + mocked-boundary integration/simulation covering checkpoint, resume, idempotence, lock lease/takeover/ownership, batch cap

16_simulation_results.result: PASS -- a deliberately tight time budget (usable window 3100ms vs 200ms/call) causes the run to voluntarily stop after exactly 1 of 4 planned units, checkpointing only that 1 unit, reporting outcome_status=time_budget_exhausted, and leaving the untouched units' rotation state completely unmarked

19_residual_risks: The new lock migration is not yet applied to Production Supabase -- next mission's precondition.
The updated code is not yet deployed to Production -- next mission's precondition.

MAX_SERVERLESS_BATCH_SIZE=5 and per-engine timeout=15s are conservative starting values chosen from first principles (no real-network timing data was available, since no real call succeeded in the incident) -- may need tuning after the first successful real run.

Vercel's own platform-level FUNCTION_INVOCATION_TIMEOUT could theoretically still fire if the safety margin (20s) proves too small under real network conditions worse than assumed -- the margin is configurable and should be revisited after real-run telemetry exists.

GitHub Actions schedule removal is a workflow-file change on this feature branch only; it must reach the default branch (main) before it has any real protective effect there -- a deploy/merge step, not yet performed by this mission (which explicitly excludes any deployment).

20_no_real_run_confirmation.production_database_modified: read-only + the one explicitly-authorized targeted DELETE (Phase 1), no other write
